description:
You are a compliance analyst who must generate an auditable, append-only trail of disk-usage measurements for a controlled data directory.

    1. Directory to audit  
       • Path: /home/user/data  
       • Only count **regular files** (exclude directories, symlinks, devices, etc.).  
       • Compute:  
         a) TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES – exact sum of the byte lengths of every regular file inside /home/user/data (all sub-directories included).  
         b) FILE_COUNT       – number of regular files inside /home/user/data (all sub-directories included).

    2. Where to record the audit entry  
       • Create (if absent) a directory /home/user/audit with default user permissions.  
       • Log file path: /home/user/audit/disk_usage_audit.log  
       • The file must be **append-only**: each new run adds exactly one new line without touching the older ones.

    3. EXACT line format that the automated checker will validate  
       • Use **UTC** timestamp produced by:  date -u "+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"  
       • One single space before and after every vertical bar (⎮).  
       • The line must look like:  

            2024-05-07T14:32:05Z | TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES=12345 | FILE_COUNT=4

       • Replace the timestamp and integer values with the actual values you compute.  
       • The trailing newline character must be present.

    4. What the task should achieve  
       • /home/user/audit exists.  
       • /home/user/audit/disk_usage_audit.log exists.  
       • The log’s **last line** follows the format above and contains the correct, current figures for /home/user/data.  
       • No other files or directories are created or modified.

    5. Typical command sequence (3–4 commands) you might perform  
       • Measure size and file count in one or two shell commands.  
       • Capture the UTC timestamp.  
       • Append the formatted line to the log file, creating parent directory/file if missing.

    The automated grader will:
       1. Recompute size and file count on its own.  
       2. Read the **last line** of /home/user/audit/disk_usage_audit.log.  
       3. Verify that:
          – The timestamp field matches the required ISO-8601 UTC layout.  
          – TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES equals the grader’s recomputed total.  
          – FILE_COUNT equals the grader’s recomputed count.  
          – Vertical bars and spacing are exactly as specified.

    Produce the correctly formatted audit line and ensure it is appended to the designated log file.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-created directory and files for the audit (owned by user:user, 0644 files, 0755 directories).

    Directory tree under /home/user/data :
       /home/user/data/fileA.txt
       /home/user/data/fileB.log
       /home/user/data/subdir1/fileC.bin
       /home/user/data/subdir2/nested.txt

    File contents (byte-exact):

    1) /home/user/data/fileA.txt
       Hello compliance\n
       (17 bytes)

    2) /home/user/data/fileB.log
       Log entry 1\n
       Log entry 2\n
       (24 bytes)

    3) /home/user/data/subdir1/fileC.bin
       1 024 zero (0x00) bytes

    4) /home/user/data/subdir2/nested.txt
       Nested compliance data\n
       (23 bytes)

    Calculated ground-truth values for verification:
       TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES = 1088
       FILE_COUNT       = 4

    The checker will recalculate these but uses the above figures as baseline.

    Initial state:
       • /home/user/audit directory does NOT exist.
       • /home/user/audit/disk_usage_audit.log does NOT exist.
       • No other relevant files/directories are present.

    Expected final state AFTER agent execution:
       • /home/user/audit exists with mode 0755 (default).
       • /home/user/audit/disk_usage_audit.log exists with at least one line.
       • The final line matches regex:
           ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z \| TOTAL_SIZE_BYTES=1088 \| FILE_COUNT=4\n$
    -->
name: task_000000_37888793
